revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=春日向山古墳 |画像=[[ファイル:Yomei tenno-ryo3200.jpg|300px]]
[[2007年]][[1月20日]]撮影 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町]]
大字春日 |位置= |形状=[[方墳]] |規模=縦63m・横60m
高さ10m・空堀7m |築造年代=[[古墳時代]]末期 |埋葬施設= |被葬者=[[用明天皇]]（宮内庁治定） |出土品= |史跡指定=河内磯長原陵（宮内庁治定） |特記事項= }} '''春日向山古墳'''（かすがむこうやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町]]大字春日にある[[古墳時代]]末期の[[古墳]]。正式陵墓名は'''河内磯長原陵'''。現在の第31代[[日本国]][[天皇]]の[[用明天皇]]陵とされる。 ==概要== 古墳は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町]]大字春日に位置する。古墳の幅は、縦63m・横60m・高さ10m・空堀7mの[[方墳]]である。古墳自体は[[古墳時代]]末期に造られたと見られる。[[埋葬|埋葬者]]は[[日本国]]の第31代[[天皇]]である[[用明天皇]][[用明天皇]]は、『[[日本書紀]]』によると、後の[[飛鳥時代]]の[[皇族]]で[[聖徳太子]]となる'''厩戸皇子'''の[[父親]]である。また厩戸皇子は、用明天皇の第二皇子として誕生している。（不明 [[欽明天皇]]元年（[[540年]]?） - 用明天皇2年[[4月9日 (旧暦)|4月9日]]（[[587年]][[5月21日]]））とみられ、日本最古の[[歴史書]]の『[[古事記]]』では「'''磐余池上陵'''に葬られ、その後春日向山古墳に陵墓を移した」と記述がある。また、'''河内磯長原陵'''の陵墓名で、[[日本]]の[[天皇陵]]として初めて[[宮内庁]]から認定を受けている。 尚、[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）[[9月29日]]に、[[宮内庁]]の天皇陵を初めとする[[古墳]]保全事業の一環として、非公式に整備作業が行われた。 ==名称== 「春日向山古墳」という名称は、古墳が発見された当時、[[名称]]が無い[[古墳]]として地元住民から、この古墳が位置する[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町]]大字春日に由来し、「春日古墳」と呼ばれる様になったと言われる。「向山」は、古墳が[[方墳]]で位置する場所が[[盆地]]から起伏しているのに由来する。 ==交通アクセス== *所在地 [[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町]]大字春日 *アクセス [[近鉄長野線]][[喜志駅]]から[[バス]]で「春日口」徒歩400m ==関連項目== *[[日本の古墳一覧#大阪府|日本の古墳一覧]] *[[:Category:日本の古墳画像|日本の古墳画像一覧]] *[[方墳]] *[[天皇陵]] *[[用明天皇]] ==外部リンク== * [http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ 宮内庁] * [http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ 宮内庁 用明天皇陵] ==脚注== {{DEFAULTSORT:かすかむこうやまこふん}} [[Category:大阪府の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府の史跡]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳|かすかむこうやま]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=春日向山古墳 |画像=[[ファイル:Yomei tenno-ryo3200.jpg|300px]]
[[2007年]][[1月20日]]撮影 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町]]
大字春日 |位置= |形状=[[方墳]] |規模=縦63m・横60m
高さ10m・空堀7m |築造年代=[[古墳時代]]末期 |埋葬施設= |被葬者=[[用明天皇]]（宮内庁治定） |出土品= |史跡指定=河内磯長原陵（宮内庁治定） |特記事項= }} [[ファイル:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kohun.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{国土航空写真}}画面右下が古墳となる]] [[ファイル:Umayado Miko.jpg|thumb|right|150px|この古墳の埋葬者である[[用明天皇]]の第二皇子である[[聖徳太子]]（厩戸皇子）]] '''春日向山古墳'''（かすがむかいやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町]]大字春日にある[[古墳時代]]末期の[[古墳]]。正式陵墓名は'''河内磯長原陵'''。現在の第31代[[日本国]][[天皇]]の[[用明天皇]]陵とされる。 ==概要== 古墳は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町]]大字春日に位置する。古墳の幅は、縦63m・横60m・高さ10m・空堀7mの[[方墳]]である。古墳自体は[[古墳時代]]末期に造られたと見られる。[[埋葬|埋葬者]]は[[日本国]]の第31代[[天皇]]である[[用明天皇]][[用明天皇]]は、『[[日本書紀]]』によると、後の[[飛鳥時代]]の[[皇族]]で[[聖徳太子]]となる'''厩戸皇子'''の[[父親]]である。また厩戸皇子は、用明天皇の第二皇子として誕生している。（不明 [[欽明天皇]]元年（[[540年]]?） - 用明天皇2年[[4月9日 (旧暦)|4月9日]]（[[587年]][[5月21日]]））とみられ、日本最古の[[歴史書]]の『[[古事記]]』では「'''磐余池上陵'''に葬られ、その後、[[推古]]1年（[[593年]]）に春日向山古墳に陵墓を移した」と記述がある。また、'''河内磯長原陵'''の陵墓名で、[[日本]]の[[天皇陵]]として初めて[[宮内庁]]から認定を受けている。 尚、[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）[[9月29日]]に、[[宮内庁]]の天皇陵を初めとする[[古墳]]保全事業の一環として、非公式に整備作業が行われた。 ==名称== 「春日向山古墳」という名称は、古墳が発見された当時、[[名称]]が無い[[古墳]]として地元住民から、この古墳が位置する[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町]]大字春日に由来し、「春日古墳」と呼ばれる様になったと言われる。「向山」は、古墳が[[方墳]]で位置する場所が[[盆地]]から起伏しているのに由来する。 ==交通アクセス== *所在地 [[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町]]大字春日 *アクセス [[近鉄長野線]][[喜志駅]]から[[バス]]で「春日口」徒歩400m *アクセス [[近鉄南大阪線]][[上ノ太子駅]]から徒歩約3km ==関連項目== *[[日本の古墳一覧#大阪府|日本の古墳一覧]] *[[:Category:日本の古墳画像|日本の古墳画像一覧]] *[[方墳]] *[[天皇陵]] *[[用明天皇]] ==外部リンク== * [http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ 宮内庁] * [http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ 宮内庁 用明天皇陵] ==脚注== {{DEFAULTSORT:かすかむこうやまこふん}} [[Category:大阪府の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府の史跡]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳|かすかむこうやま]]

[[2007年]][[1月20日]]撮影 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]
大字春日 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|0.22|N|135|38|38.703|E|}} |形状=[[方墳]] |規模=縦63m・横60m
高さ10m・空堀7m |築造年代=[[推古]]1年（[[古墳時代]]末期） |埋葬施設= |被葬者=[[用明天皇]]（宮内庁治定） |出土品= |史跡指定=河内磯長原陵（宮内庁治定） |特記事項= }} [[ファイル:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kohun.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{国土航空写真}}画面右下が古墳となる]] [[ファイル:Umayado Miko.jpg|thumb|right|150px|参考:この古墳の埋葬者の[[用明天皇]]の第二皇子である[[聖徳太子]]（厩戸皇子）]] '''春日向山古墳'''（かすがむかいやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字春日にある[[古墳時代]]末期の[[古墳]]。正式陵墓名は'''河内磯長原陵'''。現在の第31代[[日本国]][[天皇]]の[[用明天皇]][[天皇陵|陵]]とされる。 ==概要== 古墳は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字春日に位置する。古墳の幅は、縦63m・横60m・高さ10m・空堀7mの[[方墳]]である。古墳自体は[[古墳時代]]末期に造られたと見られる。[[埋葬|被葬者]]は[[日本国]]の第31代[[天皇]]である[[用明天皇]][[用明天皇]]は、『[[日本書紀]]』によると、後の[[飛鳥時代]]の[[皇族]]で[[聖徳太子]]となる'''厩戸皇子'''の[[父親]]である。また厩戸皇子は、用明天皇の第二皇子として誕生している。（不明 [[欽明天皇]]元年（[[540年]]?） - 用明天皇2年[[4月9日 (旧暦)|4月9日]]（[[587年]][[5月21日]]））とみられ、日本最古の[[歴史書]]の『[[古事記]]』では「'''磐余池上陵'''に葬られ、その後、[[推古]]1年（[[593年]]）に春日向山古墳に陵墓を移した」と記述がある。また、'''河内磯長原陵'''の陵墓名で、[[日本]]の[[天皇陵]]として初めて[[宮内庁]]から認定を受けている。 尚、[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）[[9月29日]]に、[[宮内庁]]の天皇陵を初めとする[[古墳]]保全事業の一環として、非公式に整備作業が行われた。 ==名称== 「春日向山古墳」という名称は、古墳が発見された当時、[[名称]]が無い[[古墳]]として地元住民から、この古墳が位置する[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字春日に由来し、「春日古墳」と呼ばれる様になったと言われる。「向山」は、古墳が[[方墳]]で位置する場所が[[盆地]]から起伏しているのに由来する。 ==交通アクセス== *所在地 〒583-0991 [[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字春日 *アクセス [[近鉄長野線]][[喜志駅]]から[[バス]]で「春日口」徒歩400m *アクセス [[近鉄南大阪線]][[上ノ太子駅]]から徒歩約3km ==関連項目== *[[日本の古墳一覧#大阪府|日本の古墳一覧]] *[[:Category:日本の古墳画像|日本の古墳画像一覧]] *[[方墳]] *[[天皇陵]] *[[用明天皇]] *[[石舞台古墳]] ==外部リンク== * [http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ 宮内庁] * [http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ 宮内庁 用明天皇陵] ==脚注== {{DEFAULTSORT:かすかむこうやまこふん}} [[Category:大阪府の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府の史跡]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳|かすかむこうやま]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=春日向山古墳 |画像=[[ファイル:Yomei tenno-ryo3200.jpg|300px]]
[[2007年]][[1月20日]]撮影 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字春日 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|0.22|N|135|38|38.703|E|}} |形状=[[方墳]] |規模=縦63m・横60m
高さ10m・空堀7m |築造年代=[[推古天皇]]元年（[[古墳時代]]末期） |埋葬施設= |被葬者=[[用明天皇]]（宮内庁治定） |出土品= |史跡指定=河内磯長原陵（宮内庁治定） |特記事項= }} [[ファイル:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kohun.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{国土航空写真}}画面右下が古墳]] '''春日向山古墳'''（かすがむかいやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字春日にある[[古墳時代]]末期の[[古墳]]。正式陵墓名は'''河内磯長原陵'''（かうちのしながのはらのみささぎ）。[[用明天皇]][[天皇陵|陵]]に治定される。 ==概要== 東西65m、南北60m、高さ10m、空壕幅7mの[[方墳]]で、[[古墳時代]]末期に造られたと見られる。[[埋葬|被葬者]]は[[日本国]]の第31代[[天皇]]である[[用明天皇]]（生年不明 [[欽明天皇]]元年（[[540年]]?） - 用明天皇2年[[4月9日 (旧暦)|4月9日]]（[[587年]][[5月21日]]））とみられ、日本最古の[[歴史書]]である『[[古事記]]』には、「最初に磐余池上陵に葬られ、その後、[[推古天皇]]元年（[[593年]]）に河内磯長原陵に陵墓を移した」との記述がある。 '''河内磯長原陵'''の陵墓名で、日本の[[天皇陵]]として初めて[[宮内庁]]から認定を受けている。 なお、[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）[[9月29日]]に、[[宮内庁]]の天皇陵を初めとする[[古墳]]保全事業の一環として、非公式に整備作業が行われた。 ==名称== 当古墳の発見後、古墳のある春日の地名に由来し、地元住民から、「春日古墳」と呼ばれるようになったと言われる。「向山」は、古墳が方墳であり、地面から起伏していることに由来する。 ==所在地・交通アクセス== *〒583-0991 [[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字春日 :* [[近鉄長野線]][[喜志駅]]から[[バス]]で「春日口」徒歩400m :* [[近鉄南大阪線]][[上ノ太子駅]]から徒歩約3km ==関連項目== *[[日本の古墳一覧#大阪府|日本の古墳一覧]] *[[方墳]] *[[天皇陵]] *[[用明天皇]] ==外部リンク== * [http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ 宮内庁 用明天皇陵] {{DEFAULTSORT:かすかむこうやまこふん}} [[Category:大阪府の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府の史跡]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳|かすかむこうやま]] [[Category:太子町 (大阪府)]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{日本の古墳 |名称=春日向山古墳 |画像=[[ファイル:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|300px]]
{{国土航空写真}} |所在地=[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字春日 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|0.22|N|135|38|38.703|E|}} |形状=[[方墳]] |規模=縦63m・横60m
高さ10m・空堀7m |築造年代=[[推古天皇]]元年（[[古墳時代]]末期） |埋葬施設= |被葬者=[[用明天皇]]（宮内庁治定） |出土品= |史跡指定=河内磯長原陵（宮内庁治定） |特記事項= }} [[ファイル:Yomei tenno-ryo3200.jpg|thumb|300px|河内磯長原陵拝所（[[2007年]][[1月20日]]撮影）]] '''春日向山古墳'''（かすがむかいやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字春日にある[[古墳時代]]末期の[[古墳]]。[[用明天皇]][[天皇陵|陵]]に治定され、[[天皇陵|陵墓]]名は'''河内磯長原陵'''（かうちのしながのはらのみささぎ）。 ==概要== 東西65m、南北60m、高さ10m、空壕幅7mの[[方墳]]で、[[古墳時代]]末期に造られたと見られる。[[埋葬|被葬者]]は[[日本国]]の第31代[[天皇]]である[[用明天皇]]（生年不明 [[欽明天皇]]元年（[[540年]]?） - 用明天皇2年[[4月9日 (旧暦)|4月9日]]（[[587年]][[5月21日]]））とみられ、日本最古の[[歴史書]]である『[[古事記]]』には、「最初に磐余池上陵に葬られ、その後、[[推古天皇]]元年（[[593年]]）に河内磯長原陵に陵墓を移した」との記述がある。 なお、[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）[[9月29日]]に、[[宮内庁]]の天皇陵保全事業の一環として、非公開で整備作業が行われた。 ==名称== 当古墳の発見後、古墳のある春日の地名に由来し、地元住民から、「春日古墳」と呼ばれるようになったと言われる。「向山」は、古墳が方墳であり、地面から起伏していることに由来する。 ==所在地・交通アクセス== *〒583-0991 [[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字春日 :* [[近鉄長野線]][[喜志駅]]から[[バス (交通機関)|バス]]で「春日口」徒歩400m :* [[近鉄南大阪線]][[上ノ太子駅]]から徒歩約3km ==関連項目== *[[日本の古墳一覧#大阪府|日本の古墳一覧]] *[[方墳]] *[[天皇陵]] *[[用明天皇]] ==外部リンク== * [http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ 宮内庁 用明天皇陵] {{DEFAULTSORT:かすかむこうやまこふん}} [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:大阪府の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府の史跡]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳|かすかむこうやま]] [[Category:太子町 (大阪府)]] [[Category:方墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

空堀7m |築造年代=[[推古天皇]]元年（[[古墳時代]]末期） |埋葬施設= |被葬者=第31代[[用明天皇]]（[[宮内庁]]治定） |出土品= |史跡指定=[[宮内庁]]治定「河内磯長原陵」 |特記事項= }} [[ファイル:Yomei tenno-ryo3200.jpg|thumb|300px|河内磯長原陵拝所（[[2007年]][[1月20日]]撮影）]] '''春日向山古墳'''（かすがむかいやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字春日にある[[方墳]]。[[古墳時代]]末期の[[古墳]]である。 [[宮内庁]]により「'''河内磯長原陵'''（こうちのしながのはらのみささぎ）」として第31代[[用明天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。 ==概要== 東西65m、南北60m、高さ10m、空壕幅7mの[[方墳]]で、[[古墳時代]]末期に造られたと見られる。[[埋葬|被葬者]]は[[日本国]]の第31代[[天皇]]である[[用明天皇]]（生年不明 [[欽明天皇]]元年（[[540年]]?） - 用明天皇2年[[4月9日 (旧暦)|4月9日]]（[[587年]][[5月21日]]））とみられ、日本最古の[[歴史書]]である『[[古事記]]』には、「最初に[[磐余池]]上陵に葬られ、その後、[[推古天皇]]元年（[[593年]]）に河内磯長原陵に陵墓を移した」との記述がある。 なお、[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）[[9月29日]]に、[[宮内庁]]の天皇陵保全事業の一環として、非公開で整備作業が行われた。 ==名称== 当古墳の発見後、古墳のある春日の地名に由来し、地元住民から、「春日古墳」と呼ばれるようになったと言われる。「向山」は、古墳が方墳であり、地面から起伏していることに由来する。 ==所在地・交通アクセス== *〒583-0991 [[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字春日 :* [[近鉄長野線]][[喜志駅]]から[[バス (交通機関)|バス]]で「春日口」徒歩400m :* [[近鉄南大阪線]][[上ノ太子駅]]から徒歩約3km ==関連項目== *[[日本の古墳一覧#大阪府|日本の古墳一覧]] *[[方墳]] *[[天皇陵]] *[[用明天皇]] ==外部リンク== * [http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ 河内磯長原陵] - 宮内庁 {{DEFAULTSORT:かすかむこうやまこふん}} [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳|かすかむこうやま]] [[Category:方墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 春日向山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = |所属 = [[磯長谷古墳群]] |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字春日 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 0.43 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 38|経度秒 = 39.13 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = [[方墳]] |規模 = 東西65m×南北60m
高さ10m |築造年代 = [[7世紀]]前半 |埋葬施設 = （推定）[[横穴式石室]] |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）第31代[[用明天皇]] |出土品 = |陵墓 = 宮内庁治定「河内磯長原陵」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Osaka |ラベル = 春日向山
古墳 |アイコン = 方墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|34|30|58|N|135|38|43|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 320| height =250 | caption = {{center|[[磯長谷古墳群]]分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|30|42.60|N|135|38|7.75|E}} | label1=太子西山 (敏達) | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-北西.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|31|0.43|N|135|38|39.13|E}} | label2=春日向山 (用明) | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|30|42.83|N|135|38|58.44|E}} | label3=山田高塚 (推古) | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=方墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|31|3.14|N|135|39|15.84|E}} | label4=山田上ノ山 (孝徳) | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|31|11.23|N|135|38|21.38|E}} | label5=叡福寺北 (聖徳太子) | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|34|30|38.96|N|135|39|5.65|E}} | label6=二子塚 | label-pos6=bottom | label-color6=black | mark6=方墳.png | mark-size6=30 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|34|30|41.09|N|135|38|32.64|E}} | label7=釜戸塚 | label-pos7=top | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.png | mark-size7=30 | mark-coord8= {{Coord|34|30|38.94|N|135|38|33.84|E}} | label8=石塚 | label-pos8=bottom | label-color8=black | mark8=円墳.png | mark-size8=30 | mark-coord9= {{Coord|34|30|40.17|N|135|38|36.33|E}} | label9=葉室塚 | label-pos9=right | label-color9=black | mark9=方墳.png | mark-size9=30 }} [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[用明天皇]]河内磯長原陵 拝所}}]] '''春日向山古墳'''（かすがむかいやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字春日にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[方墳]]。[[磯長谷古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''河内磯長原陵'''（こうちのしながのはらのみささぎ）」として第31代[[用明天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|春日向山古墳の航空写真
（1985年度）}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] 大阪府南東部、[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]山麓の磯長谷において、春日集落の南西端の台地上に築造された大型方墳である{{Sfn|向山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}。現在は[[宮内庁]]治定の天皇陵として同庁の管理下にあるため、これまでに本格的な調査はなされていないが、[[2012年]]（[[平成]]24年）2月に学会立ち入り調査が実施されている[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "用明陵に立ち入り調査 大阪、研究者の団体"]（日本経済新聞、2012年2月23日記事）。。 墳形は方形で、南方に面する。墳丘は3段築成と推測され{{Sfn|用明天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}、東西65メートル、南北60メートル、高さ10メートルを測る{{Sfn|用明天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html 用明天皇陵]（太子町ホームページ）。。墳丘周囲には空壕（幅7メートル）が巡らされており、壕の外堤（土塁）まで含めた古墳全長は東西100メートル、南北90メートルにもおよぶ{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=15-16}}。墳丘表面で[[埴輪]]は認められていない{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=15-16}}。埋葬施設は明らかでないが、[[享保]]10年（[[1725年]]）の古文書（春日の吉村家蔵文書）では[[横穴式石室]]の可能性を示唆する記述があるほか{{Sfn|向山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=15-16}}、『廟陵記』にも「大石有之」として巨石（横穴式石室の部材か）の露出が記される{{Sfn|用明天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。 この春日向山古墳は、[[古墳時代]]終末期の[[7世紀]]前半頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|用明天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。古墳形態としては[[石舞台古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]、[[蘇我馬子]]墓か）との関連が指摘される（同一プランの築造か）{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=15-16}}。被葬者は明らかでないが、現在は[[宮内庁]]により第31代[[用明天皇]]（[[587年]]?崩御）の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}。磯長谷では用明天皇陵のほか[[敏達天皇|敏達]]・[[推古天皇|推古]]・[[孝徳天皇]]陵と[[聖徳太子]]墓が伝わっており、これらは「梅鉢御陵」と総称される{{Sfn|向山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}。 == 被葬者 == 春日向山古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第31代'''[[用明天皇]]'''の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定している[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html 天皇陵]（宮内庁）。宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/12}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）11コマ。{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}『陵墓地形図集成 縮小版』 宮内庁書陵部陵墓課編、学生社、2014年、p. 407。。用明天皇について、『[[日本書紀]]』では用明天皇2年（[[587年]]?）4月『日本書紀』用明天皇2年（587年?）4月癸丑（9日）条。に崩御し、同年7月『日本書紀』用明天皇2年（587年?）7月甲午（21日）条。に「磐余池上陵」に葬られたのち、[[推古天皇]]元年（[[593年]]）『日本書紀』推古天皇元年（593年）9月条。に「河内磯長陵」に改葬されたとする{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}『新編日本古典文学全集 3 日本書紀 (2)』小学館、2004年（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]版）、p. 532。。また『[[古事記]]』では「御陵在石寸掖上、後遷科長中陵也」と見える（掖上は池上の誤写か『新編日本古典文学全集 3 日本書紀 (2)』小学館、2004年（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]版）、pp. 507-508。）。『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]『延喜式』巻21（治部省）諸陵寮条。では用明天皇陵は遠陵の「河内磯長原陵」として記載され、[[河内国]][[石川郡 (大阪府)|石川郡]]の所在で、兆域は東西2町・南北3町で守戸3烟を毎年あてるとする{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}。その後、[[元治]]元年（[[1864年]]）に修補および拝所設置が実施されたのち、[[1899年]]（[[明治]]32年）には御在所修理が実施されている{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}。 本古墳が用明天皇陵であれば、天皇陵としては最初の方形墳に位置づけられる{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=15-16}}。なお、初葬地の「磐余池上陵」については明らかでないが{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}、一説には[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]阿部付近に推定される{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}。 {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;white-space:nowrap;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+梅鉢御陵関係系図 |- |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|~|y|~|~|~|~|~|~|~|~|~|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01=[[石姫皇女|{{color|#FC4E6B|'''石姫'''}}]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 春日向山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = |所属 = [[磯長谷古墳群]] |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字春日 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 0.43 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 38|経度秒 = 39.13 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = [[方墳]] |規模 = 東西65m×南北60m
高さ10m |築造年代 = [[7世紀]]前半 |埋葬施設 = （推定）[[横穴式石室]] |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）第31代[[用明天皇]] |出土品 = |陵墓 = 宮内庁治定「河内磯長原陵」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Osaka |ラベル = 春日向山
古墳 |アイコン = 方墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|34|30|58|N|135|38|43|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 320| height =250 | caption = {{center|[[磯長谷古墳群]]分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|30|42.60|N|135|38|7.75|E}} | label1=太子西山 (敏達) | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-北西.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|31|0.43|N|135|38|39.13|E}} | label2=春日向山 (用明) | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|30|42.83|N|135|38|58.44|E}} | label3=山田高塚 (推古) | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=方墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|31|3.14|N|135|39|15.84|E}} | label4=山田上ノ山 (孝徳) | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|31|11.23|N|135|38|21.38|E}} | label5=叡福寺北 (聖徳太子) | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|34|30|38.96|N|135|39|5.65|E}} | label6=二子塚 | label-pos6=bottom | label-color6=black | mark6=方墳.png | mark-size6=30 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|34|30|41.09|N|135|38|32.64|E}} | label7=釜戸塚 | label-pos7=top | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.png | mark-size7=30 | mark-coord8= {{Coord|34|30|38.94|N|135|38|33.84|E}} | label8=石塚 | label-pos8=bottom | label-color8=black | mark8=円墳.png | mark-size8=30 | mark-coord9= {{Coord|34|30|40.17|N|135|38|36.33|E}} | label9=葉室塚 | label-pos9=right | label-color9=black | mark9=方墳.png | mark-size9=30 }} [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[用明天皇]]河内磯長原陵 拝所}}]] '''春日向山古墳'''（かすがむかいやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字春日にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[方墳]]。[[磯長谷古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''河内磯長原陵'''（こうちのしながのはらのみささぎ）」として第31代[[用明天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|春日向山古墳の航空写真
（1985年度）}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] 大阪府南東部、[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]山麓の磯長谷において、春日集落の南西端の台地上に築造された大型方墳である{{Sfn|向山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}。現在は[[宮内庁]]治定の天皇陵として同庁の管理下にあるため、これまでに本格的な調査はなされていないが、[[2012年]]（[[平成]]24年）2月に学会立ち入り調査が実施されている[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "用明陵に立ち入り調査 大阪、研究者の団体"]（日本経済新聞、2012年2月23日記事）。。 墳形は方形で、南方に面する。墳丘は3段築成と推測され{{Sfn|用明天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}、東西65メートル、南北60メートル、高さ10メートルを測る{{Sfn|用明天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html 用明天皇陵]（太子町ホームページ）。。墳丘周囲には空壕（幅7メートル）が巡らされており、壕の外堤（土塁）まで含めた古墳全長は東西100メートル、南北90メートルにもおよぶ{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=15-16}}。墳丘外表で[[埴輪]]は認められていない{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=15-16}}。埋葬施設は明らかでないが、[[享保]]10年（[[1725年]]）の古文書（春日の吉村家蔵文書）では[[横穴式石室]]の可能性を示唆する記述があるほか{{Sfn|向山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=15-16}}、『廟陵記』にも「大石有之」として巨石（横穴式石室の部材か）の露出が記される{{Sfn|用明天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。 この春日向山古墳は、[[古墳時代]]終末期の[[7世紀]]前半頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|用明天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。古墳形態としては[[石舞台古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]、[[蘇我馬子]]墓か）との関連が指摘される（同一プランの築造か）{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=15-16}}。被葬者は明らかでないが、現在は[[宮内庁]]により第31代[[用明天皇]]（[[587年]]?崩御）の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}。磯長谷では用明天皇陵のほか[[敏達天皇|敏達]]・[[推古天皇|推古]]・[[孝徳天皇]]陵と[[聖徳太子]]墓が伝わっており、これらは「梅鉢御陵」と総称される{{Sfn|向山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}。 == 被葬者 == 春日向山古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第31代'''[[用明天皇]]'''の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定している[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html 天皇陵]（宮内庁）。宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/12}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）11コマ。{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}『陵墓地形図集成 縮小版』 宮内庁書陵部陵墓課編、学生社、2014年、p. 407。。用明天皇について、『[[日本書紀]]』では用明天皇2年（[[587年]]?）4月『日本書紀』用明天皇2年（587年?）4月癸丑（9日）条。に崩御し、同年7月『日本書紀』用明天皇2年（587年?）7月甲午（21日）条。に「磐余池上陵」に葬られたのち、[[推古天皇]]元年（[[593年]]）『日本書紀』推古天皇元年（593年）9月条。に「河内磯長陵」に改葬されたとする{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}『新編日本古典文学全集 3 日本書紀 (2)』小学館、2004年（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]版）、p. 532。。また『[[古事記]]』では「御陵在石寸掖上、後遷科長中陵也」と見える（掖上は池上の誤写か『新編日本古典文学全集 3 日本書紀 (2)』小学館、2004年（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]版）、pp. 507-508。）。『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]『延喜式』巻21（治部省）諸陵寮条。では用明天皇陵は遠陵の「河内磯長原陵」として記載され、[[河内国]][[石川郡 (大阪府)|石川郡]]の所在で、兆域は東西2町・南北3町で守戸3烟を毎年あてるとする{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}。その後、[[元治]]元年（[[1864年]]）に修補および拝所設置が実施されたのち、[[1899年]]（[[明治]]32年）には御在所修理が実施されている{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}。 本古墳が用明天皇陵であれば、天皇陵としては最初の方形墳に位置づけられる{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=15-16}}。なお、初葬地の「磐余池上陵」については明らかでないが{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}、一説には[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]阿部付近に推定される{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}。 {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;white-space:nowrap;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+梅鉢御陵関係系図 |- |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} revision.text:
{{Infobox burial mound | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Burial mound | alternative_name = | group = {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | construction_period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Burial Mound | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is a large square-shaped burial mound constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website [[Category:Shinagadani Burial Mound Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square burial mounds]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:春日向山古墳]]

No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website [[Category:Shinagadani Burial Mound Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square burial mounds]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[ja:春日向山古墳]]

The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. (1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}} The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is a large square-shaped burial mound constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website [[Category:Shinagadani Burial Mound Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square burial mounds]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[ja:春日向山古墳]]

{{Infobox burial mound | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Burial mound | alternative_name = | group = {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | construction_period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Burial Mound | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is a large square-shaped burial mound constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website [[Category:Shinagadani Burial Mound Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square burial mounds]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:春日向山古墳]]

However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website [[Category:Shinagadani Burial Mound Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square burial mounds]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[ja:春日向山古墳]]

{{Infobox burial mound | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Burial mound | alternative_name = | group = {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | construction_period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Burial Mound | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is a large square-shaped burial mound constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website [[Category:Shinagadani Burial Mound Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square burial mounds]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[ja:春日向山古墳]]

Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - {{Infobox burial mound | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Burial mound | alternative_name = | group = {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | construction_period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Burial Mound | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is a large square-shaped burial mound constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website [[Category:Shinagadani Burial Mound Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square burial mounds]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:春日向山古墳]]

Height: 10m | construction_period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Burial Mound | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is a large square-shaped burial mound constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website [[Category:Shinagadani Burial Mound Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square burial mounds]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:春日向山古墳]]

Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website [[Category:Shinagadani Burial Mound Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square burial mounds]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[ja:春日向山古墳]]

{{Infobox burial mound | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Burial mound | alternative_name = | group = {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | construction_period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Burial Mound | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is a large square-shaped burial mound constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website [[Category:Shinagadani Burial Mound Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square burial mounds]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:春日向山古墳]]

The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is a large square-shaped burial mound constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website [[Category:Shinagadani Burial Mound Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square burial mounds]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:春日向山古墳]]

The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] -
