Burial mound | alternative_name = | group = {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | construction_period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Burial Mound | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is a large square-shaped burial mound constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - (1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is a large square-shaped burial mound constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - {{Infobox burial mound | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Burial mound | alternative_name = | group = {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | construction_period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Burial Mound | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is a large square-shaped burial mound constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website [[Category:Shinagadani Burial Mound Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square burial mounds]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasugayama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasugayama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasugayama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:春日向山古墳]]

{{Infobox burial mound | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Burial mound | alternative_name = | group = {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | construction_period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Burial Mound | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is a large square-shaped burial mound constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website [[Category:Shinagadani Burial Mound Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square burial mounds]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasugayama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasugayama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasugayama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:春日向山古墳]] [[Category:ppp]]

{{Infobox burial mound | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Burial mound | alternative_name = | group = {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | construction_period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Burial Mound | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is a large square-shaped burial mound constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - {{Infobox burial mound | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Burial mound | alternative_name = | group = {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | construction_period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Burial Mound | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is a large square-shaped burial mound constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website [[Category:Shinagadani Burial Mound Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square burial mounds]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasugayama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasugayama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasugayama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:春日向山古墳]] [[Category:ppp]] [[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:横穴式石室]]

The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - {{Infobox burial mound | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Burial mound | alternative_name = | group = {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | construction_period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Burial Mound | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is a large square-shaped burial mound constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website [[Category:Shinagadani Burial Mound Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square burial mounds]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasugayama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasugayama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasugayama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:春日向山古墳]] [[Category:ppp]] [[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well {{Infobox burial mound | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Burial mound | alternative_name = | group = {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | construction_period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Burial Mound | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is a large square-shaped burial mound constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite {{Infobox burial mound | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Burial mound | alternative_name = | group = {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | construction_period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Burial Mound | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is a large square-shaped burial mound constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite {{Infobox burial mound | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Burial mound | alternative_name = | group = {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | construction_period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Burial Mound | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is a large square-shaped burial mound constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite {{Infobox burial mound | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Burial mound | alternative_name = | group = {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | construction_period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Burial Mound | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is a large square-shaped burial mound constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite {{Infobox burial mound | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Burial mound | alternative_name = | group = {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | construction_period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Burial Mound | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Burial Mound Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is a large square-shaped burial mound constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Burial Mound|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Burial Mound is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Burial Mound Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite
