Height: 10m | construction_period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Kofun | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | alternative_name = | group = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | construction_period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Kofun | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | alternative_name = | group = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | construction_period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Kofun | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | alternative_name = | group = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | construction_period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Kofun | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-scale excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Kofun | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-map_size excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Imperial Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun part_of, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Kofun | alternative_name = | group = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Kofun | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} and has a square (''hōfun'') shape. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-map_size excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in shape and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Kofun | alternative_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Kofun | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} and has a square (''hōfun'') type. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-typed Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-map_size excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in type and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-typed imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun revision.comment: trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | alternative_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Kofun | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} and has a square (''hōfun'') type. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-map_size excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in type and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Kofun | alternative_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Kofun | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} and has a square (''hōfun'') type. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-map_size excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in type and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | alternative_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Kofun | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} and has a square (''hōfun'') type. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-map_size excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in type and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | alternative_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 0.43 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} | size = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Kofun | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} and has a square (''hōfun'') type. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-map_size excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in type and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Height: 10m | built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | burial_facility = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | grave_goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Kofun | icon = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} and has a square (''hōfun'') type. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designation1 this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-map_size excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in type and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Kofun | type = Square mound | label_position = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} and has a square (''hōfun'') type. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designation1 this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-map_size excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in type and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Kofun | type = Square mound | map_dot_map_dot_label = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} and has a square (''hōfun'') type. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has other_designation1 this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Intypeation (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, no full-map_size excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in type and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 28: Osaka
