However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft|12=draft}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵|12=draft|12=draft}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]] [[ja:春日向山古墳]] [[en:Kasugayama Kofun]]

Kofun | type = Square mound | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft|12=draft}} and has a square (''hōfun'') type. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} has Historic Site1 this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Intypeation (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft|12=draft}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}}, no full-map_size excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in type and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft|12=draft}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵|12=draft|12=draft}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in type and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft|12=draft}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵|12=draft|12=draft}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]] [[ja:春日向山古墳]] [[en:Kasugayama Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 31 | coordinates = 0.43 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 38 | coordinates = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} | burial = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Kofun | type = Square mound | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft|12=draft}} and has a square (''hōfun'') type. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} has Historic Site1 this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Intypeation (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft|12=draft}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}}, no full-map_size excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in type and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft|12=draft}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵|12=draft|12=draft}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]] [[ja:春日向山古墳]] [[en:Kasugayama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Kasugayama Kofun]] to [[Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 31 | coordinates = 0.43 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 38 | coordinates = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} | burial = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Kofun | type = Square mound | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft|12=draft}} and has a square (''hōfun'') type. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} has Historic Site1 this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Intypeation (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft|12=draft}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}}, no full-map_size excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in type and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft|12=draft}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵|12=draft|12=draft}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]] [[ja:春日向山古墳]] [[en:Kasugayama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft|12=draft}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵|12=draft|12=draft}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]] [[ja:春日向山古墳]] [[en:Kasugayama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Kofun | type = Square mound | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} and has a square (''hōfun'') type. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} has Historic Site1 this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Intypeation (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft|12=draft}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}}, no full-map_size excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in type and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft|12=draft}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵|12=draft|12=draft}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - (1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Intypeation (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft|12=draft}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}}, no full-map_size excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in type and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft|12=draft}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Buried Individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵|12=draft|12=draft}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]] [[ja:春日向山古墳]] [[en:Kasugayama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.comment: fixing unknown parameters from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 31 | coordinates = 0.43 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 38 | coordinates = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} | burial = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Kofun | type = Square mound | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} and has a square (''hōfun'') type. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} has Historic Site1 this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Intypeation (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft|12=draft}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}}, no full-map_size excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in type and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft|12=draft}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Interred_individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵|12=draft|12=draft}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]] [[ja:春日向山古墳]] [[en:Kasugayama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}}

34 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:春日向山古墳 up to rev 93995111
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 春日向山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = |所属 = [[磯長谷古墳群]] |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字春日 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 0.43 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 38|経度秒 = 39.13 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = [[方墳]] |規模 = 東西65m×南北60m
高さ10m |築造年代 = [[7世紀]]前半 |埋葬施設 = （推定）[[横穴式石室]] |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）第31代[[用明天皇]] |出土品 = |陵墓 = 宮内庁治定「河内磯長原陵」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Osaka |ラベル = 春日向山
古墳 |アイコン = 方墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|34|30|58|N|135|38|43|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 320| height =250 | caption = {{center|[[磯長谷古墳群]]分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|30|42.60|N|135|38|7.75|E}} | label1=太子西山 (敏達) | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-北西.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|31|0.43|N|135|38|39.13|E}} | label2=春日向山 (用明) | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|30|42.83|N|135|38|58.44|E}} | label3=山田高塚 (推古) | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=方墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|31|3.14|N|135|39|15.84|E}} | label4=山田上ノ山 (孝徳) | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|31|11.23|N|135|38|21.38|E}} | label5=叡福寺北 (聖徳太子) | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|34|30|38.96|N|135|39|5.65|E}} | label6=二子塚 | label-pos6=bottom | label-color6=black | mark6=方墳.png | mark-size6=30 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|34|30|41.09|N|135|38|32.64|E}} | label7=釜戸塚 | label-pos7=top | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.png | mark-size7=30 | mark-coord8= {{Coord|34|30|38.94|N|135|38|33.84|E}} | label8=石塚 | label-pos8=bottom | label-color8=black | mark8=円墳.png | mark-size8=30 | mark-coord9= {{Coord|34|30|40.17|N|135|38|36.33|E}} | label9=葉室塚 | label-pos9=right | label-color9=black | mark9=方墳.png | mark-size9=30 }} [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[用明天皇]]河内磯長原陵 拝所}}]] '''春日向山古墳'''（かすがむかいやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字春日にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[方墳]]。[[磯長谷古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''河内磯長原陵'''（こうちのしながのはらのみささぎ）」として第31代[[用明天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|春日向山古墳の航空写真
（1985年度）}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] 大阪府南東部、[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]山麓の磯長谷において、春日集落の南西端の台地上に築造された大型方墳である{{Sfn|向山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}。現在は[[宮内庁]]治定の天皇陵として同庁の管理下にあるため、これまでに本格的な調査はなされていないが、[[2012年]]（[[平成]]24年）2月に学会立ち入り調査が実施されている[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "用明陵に立ち入り調査 大阪、研究者の団体"]（日本経済新聞、2012年2月23日記事）。。 墳形は方形で、南方に面する。墳丘は3段築成と推測され{{Sfn|用明天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}、東西65メートル、南北60メートル、高さ10メートルを測る{{Sfn|用明天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html 用明天皇陵]（太子町ホームページ）。。墳丘周囲には空壕（幅7メートル）が巡らされており、壕の外堤（土塁）まで含めた古墳全長は東西100メートル、南北90メートルにもおよぶ{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=15-16}}。墳丘外表で[[埴輪]]は認められていない{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=15-16}}。埋葬施設は明らかでないが、[[享保]]10年（[[1725年]]）の古文書（春日の吉村家蔵文書）では[[横穴式石室]]の可能性を示唆する記述があるほか{{Sfn|向山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=15-16}}、『廟陵記』にも「大石有之」として巨石（横穴式石室の部材か）の露出が記される{{Sfn|用明天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。 この春日向山古墳は、[[古墳時代]]終末期の[[7世紀]]前半頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|用明天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。古墳形態としては[[石舞台古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]、[[蘇我馬子]]墓か）との関連が指摘される（同一プランの築造か）{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=15-16}}。被葬者は明らかでないが、現在は[[宮内庁]]により第31代[[用明天皇]]（[[587年]]?崩御）の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}。磯長谷では用明天皇陵のほか[[敏達天皇|敏達]]・[[推古天皇|推古]]・[[孝徳天皇]]陵と[[聖徳太子]]墓が伝わっており、これらは「梅鉢御陵」と総称される{{Sfn|向山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}。 == 被葬者 == 春日向山古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第31代'''[[用明天皇]]'''の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定している[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html 天皇陵]（宮内庁）。宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/12}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）11コマ。{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}『陵墓地形図集成 縮小版』 宮内庁書陵部陵墓課編、学生社、2014年、p. 407。。用明天皇について、『[[日本書紀]]』では用明天皇2年（[[587年]]?）4月『日本書紀』用明天皇2年（587年?）4月癸丑（9日）条。に崩御し、同年7月『日本書紀』用明天皇2年（587年?）7月甲午（21日）条。に「磐余池上陵」に葬られたのち、[[推古天皇]]元年（[[593年]]）『日本書紀』推古天皇元年（593年）9月条。に「河内磯長陵」に改葬されたとする{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}『新編日本古典文学全集 3 日本書紀 (2)』小学館、2004年（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]版）、p. 532。。また『[[古事記]]』では「御陵在石寸掖上、後遷科長中陵也」と見える（掖上は池上の誤写か『新編日本古典文学全集 3 日本書紀 (2)』小学館、2004年（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]版）、pp. 507-508。）。『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]『延喜式』巻21（治部省）諸陵寮条。では用明天皇陵は遠陵の「河内磯長原陵」として記載され、[[河内国]][[石川郡 (大阪府)|石川郡]]の所在で、兆域は東西2町・南北3町で守戸3烟を毎年あてるとする{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}。その後、[[元治]]元年（[[1864年]]）に修補および拝所設置が実施されたのち、[[1899年]]（[[明治]]32年）には御在所修理が実施されている{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}。 本古墳が用明天皇陵であれば、天皇陵としては最初の方形墳に位置づけられる{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=15-16}}。なお、初葬地の「磐余池上陵」については明らかでないが{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}、一説には[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]阿部付近に推定される{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}。 {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;white-space:nowrap;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+梅鉢御陵関係系図 |- |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{Supra|37}} [[斉明天皇|{{color|#FC4E6B|斉明天皇}}]]|03={{Supra|34}} [[舒明天皇]]}} {{familytree/end}} |} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''原典''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=[[遠山美都男]] 「用明天皇」|ref={{Harvid|用明天皇（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=石田茂輔 「河内磯長原陵」（用明天皇項目内）|ref={{Harvid|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=向山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 28 大阪府の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|向山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=高島徹|year=1989|chapter=用明天皇陵古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|用明天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=用明天皇陵|title=王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群 -太子町の古墳墓-|publisher=太子町教育委員会（[[太子町立竹内街道歴史資料館]]）|pages=15-16|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[用明天皇]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ 河内磯長原陵] - 宮内庁 * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html 用明天皇陵] - 太子町ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:かすかむかいやまこふん}} [[Category:磯長谷古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:方墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:用明天皇]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[春日向山古墳]] to [[Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 春日向山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = |所属 = [[磯長谷古墳群]] |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字春日 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 0.43 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 38|経度秒 = 39.13 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = [[方墳]] |規模 = 東西65m×南北60m
高さ10m |築造年代 = [[7世紀]]前半 |埋葬施設 = （推定）[[横穴式石室]] |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）第31代[[用明天皇]] |出土品 = |陵墓 = 宮内庁治定「河内磯長原陵」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Osaka |ラベル = 春日向山
古墳 |アイコン = 方墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|34|30|58|N|135|38|43|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 320| height =250 | caption = {{center|[[磯長谷古墳群]]分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|30|42.60|N|135|38|7.75|E}} | label1=太子西山 (敏達) | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-北西.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|31|0.43|N|135|38|39.13|E}} | label2=春日向山 (用明) | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=方墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|30|42.83|N|135|38|58.44|E}} | label3=山田高塚 (推古) | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=方墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|31|3.14|N|135|39|15.84|E}} | label4=山田上ノ山 (孝徳) | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|31|11.23|N|135|38|21.38|E}} | label5=叡福寺北 (聖徳太子) | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|34|30|38.96|N|135|39|5.65|E}} | label6=二子塚 | label-pos6=bottom | label-color6=black | mark6=方墳.png | mark-size6=30 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|34|30|41.09|N|135|38|32.64|E}} | label7=釜戸塚 | label-pos7=top | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.png | mark-size7=30 | mark-coord8= {{Coord|34|30|38.94|N|135|38|33.84|E}} | label8=石塚 | label-pos8=bottom | label-color8=black | mark8=円墳.png | mark-size8=30 | mark-coord9= {{Coord|34|30|40.17|N|135|38|36.33|E}} | label9=葉室塚 | label-pos9=right | label-color9=black | mark9=方墳.png | mark-size9=30 }} [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[用明天皇]]河内磯長原陵 拝所}}]] '''春日向山古墳'''（かすがむかいやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字春日にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[方墳]]。[[磯長谷古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''河内磯長原陵'''（こうちのしながのはらのみささぎ）」として第31代[[用明天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|春日向山古墳の航空写真
（1985年度）}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] 大阪府南東部、[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]山麓の磯長谷において、春日集落の南西端の台地上に築造された大型方墳である{{Sfn|向山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}。現在は[[宮内庁]]治定の天皇陵として同庁の管理下にあるため、これまでに本格的な調査はなされていないが、[[2012年]]（[[平成]]24年）2月に学会立ち入り調査が実施されている[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "用明陵に立ち入り調査 大阪、研究者の団体"]（日本経済新聞、2012年2月23日記事）。。 墳形は方形で、南方に面する。墳丘は3段築成と推測され{{Sfn|用明天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}、東西65メートル、南北60メートル、高さ10メートルを測る{{Sfn|用明天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html 用明天皇陵]（太子町ホームページ）。。墳丘周囲には空壕（幅7メートル）が巡らされており、壕の外堤（土塁）まで含めた古墳全長は東西100メートル、南北90メートルにもおよぶ{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=15-16}}。墳丘外表で[[埴輪]]は認められていない{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=15-16}}。埋葬施設は明らかでないが、[[享保]]10年（[[1725年]]）の古文書（春日の吉村家蔵文書）では[[横穴式石室]]の可能性を示唆する記述があるほか{{Sfn|向山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=15-16}}、『廟陵記』にも「大石有之」として巨石（横穴式石室の部材か）の露出が記される{{Sfn|用明天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。 この春日向山古墳は、[[古墳時代]]終末期の[[7世紀]]前半頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|用明天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。古墳形態としては[[石舞台古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]、[[蘇我馬子]]墓か）との関連が指摘される（同一プランの築造か）{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=15-16}}。被葬者は明らかでないが、現在は[[宮内庁]]により第31代[[用明天皇]]（[[587年]]?崩御）の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}。磯長谷では用明天皇陵のほか[[敏達天皇|敏達]]・[[推古天皇|推古]]・[[孝徳天皇]]陵と[[聖徳太子]]墓が伝わっており、これらは「梅鉢御陵」と総称される{{Sfn|向山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}。 == 被葬者 == 春日向山古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第31代'''[[用明天皇]]'''の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定している[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html 天皇陵]（宮内庁）。宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/12}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）11コマ。{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}『陵墓地形図集成 縮小版』 宮内庁書陵部陵墓課編、学生社、2014年、p. 407。。用明天皇について、『[[日本書紀]]』では用明天皇2年（[[587年]]?）4月『日本書紀』用明天皇2年（587年?）4月癸丑（9日）条。に崩御し、同年7月『日本書紀』用明天皇2年（587年?）7月甲午（21日）条。に「磐余池上陵」に葬られたのち、[[推古天皇]]元年（[[593年]]）『日本書紀』推古天皇元年（593年）9月条。に「河内磯長陵」に改葬されたとする{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}『新編日本古典文学全集 3 日本書紀 (2)』小学館、2004年（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]版）、p. 532。。また『[[古事記]]』では「御陵在石寸掖上、後遷科長中陵也」と見える（掖上は池上の誤写か『新編日本古典文学全集 3 日本書紀 (2)』小学館、2004年（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]版）、pp. 507-508。）。『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]『延喜式』巻21（治部省）諸陵寮条。では用明天皇陵は遠陵の「河内磯長原陵」として記載され、[[河内国]][[石川郡 (大阪府)|石川郡]]の所在で、兆域は東西2町・南北3町で守戸3烟を毎年あてるとする{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}。その後、[[元治]]元年（[[1864年]]）に修補および拝所設置が実施されたのち、[[1899年]]（[[明治]]32年）には御在所修理が実施されている{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}。 本古墳が用明天皇陵であれば、天皇陵としては最初の方形墳に位置づけられる{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=15-16}}。なお、初葬地の「磐余池上陵」については明らかでないが{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}、一説には[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]阿部付近に推定される{{Sfn|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}。 {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;white-space:nowrap;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+梅鉢御陵関係系図 |- |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{Supra|37}} [[斉明天皇|{{color|#FC4E6B|斉明天皇}}]]|03={{Supra|34}} [[舒明天皇]]}} {{familytree/end}} |} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''原典''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=[[遠山美都男]] 「用明天皇」|ref={{Harvid|用明天皇（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=石田茂輔 「河内磯長原陵」（用明天皇項目内）|ref={{Harvid|河内磯長原陵（国史）}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=向山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 28 大阪府の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|向山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=高島徹|year=1989|chapter=用明天皇陵古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|用明天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=用明天皇陵|title=王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群 -太子町の古墳墓-|publisher=太子町教育委員会（[[太子町立竹内街道歴史資料館]]）|pages=15-16|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[用明天皇]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ 河内磯長原陵] - 宮内庁 * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html 用明天皇陵] - 太子町ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:かすかむかいやまこふん}} [[Category:磯長谷古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:方墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:用明天皇]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 31 | coordinates = 0.43 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 38 | coordinates = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} | burial = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Kofun | type = Square mound | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and has a square (''hōfun'') type. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} has Historic Site1 this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Intypeation (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, no full-map_size excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in type and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Interred_individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|春日向山古墳|version=93995111|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial mausoleums in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:Kasugayama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Kasugayama Kofun]] [[ja:春日向山古墳]] [[en:Kasugayama Kofun]]
