The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|en|Ishibutai Kofun|WD=Q3816159|lt=Ishibutai Kofun}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Interred_individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|春日向山古墳|version=93995111|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:春日向山古墳]] {{Commons category|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11513590}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taishi, Ōsaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]][[ja:春日向山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]][[ja:春日向山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]][[ja:春日向山古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]][[ja:春日向山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|en|Taishi, Osaka|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 31 | coordinates = 0.43 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 38 | coordinates = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | built = Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} | burial = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokona-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Kofun | type = Square mound | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|en|Taishi, Osaka|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and has a square (''hōfun'') type. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Intypeation (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}}, no full-map_size excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in type and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|en|Ishibutai Kofun|WD=Q3816159|lt=Ishibutai Kofun}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Interred_individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - [[co:commons:Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]][[ja:春日向山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]][[ja:春日向山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]][[ja:春日向山古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]][[ja:春日向山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|en|Taishi, Osaka|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 31 | coordinates = 0.43 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 38 | coordinates = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | built = Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} | burial = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokona-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Kofun | type = Square mound | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|en|Taishi, Osaka|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and has a square (''hōfun'') type. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Intypeation (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}}, no full-map_size excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in type and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|en|Ishibutai Kofun|WD=Q3816159|lt=Ishibutai Kofun}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Interred_individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|春日向山古墳|version=93995111|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:春日向山古墳]] {{Commons category|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11513590}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taishi, Ōsaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Intypeation (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and has a square (''hōfun'') type. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Intypeation (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}, no full-map_size excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in type and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|en|Ishibutai Kofun|WD=Q3816159|lt=Ishibutai Kofun|1=Ishibutai Kofun}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara|1=Asuka, Nara}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Interred_individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=National History Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei|1=Emperor Yōmei}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|en|Ishibutai Kofun|WD=Q3816159|lt=Ishibutai Kofun|1=Ishibutai Kofun}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara|1=Asuka, Nara}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Interred_individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=National History Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei|1=Emperor Yōmei}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Kofun | type = Square mound | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|en|Taishi, Osaka|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi, Osaka|1=Taishi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and has a square (''hōfun'') type. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Intypeation (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}, no full-map_size excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in type and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|en|Ishibutai Kofun|WD=Q3816159|lt=Ishibutai Kofun|1=Ishibutai Kofun}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara|1=Asuka, Nara}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Interred_individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=National History Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei|1=Emperor Yōmei}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|en|Ishibutai Kofun|qid=Q3816159|lt=Ishibutai Kofun|1=Ishibutai Kofun}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|qid=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara|1=Asuka, Nara}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Interred_individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=National History Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6426904}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4346755}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17213509}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|qid=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei|1=Emperor Yōmei}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|春日向山古墳|version=93995111|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:春日向山古墳]] {{Commons category|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11513590}} [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taishi, Ōsaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]][[ja:春日向山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]][[ja:春日向山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun]][[ja:春日向山古墳]] [[ja:春日向山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|en|Taishi, Osaka|qid=Q1347173|lt=Taishi, Osaka|1=Taishi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 31 | coordinates = 0.43 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 38 | coordinates = 39.13 | iso = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = 65m (east-west) × 60m (north-south)
Height: 10m | built = Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} | burial = Presumed {{ill|yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokona-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | buried_person = Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} as Emperor Yōmei (31st Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = | imperial_tomb = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi" | notable = | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Kasuga Mukaiyama
Kofun | type = Square mound | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun''' (春日向山古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|en|Taishi, Osaka|qid=Q1347173|lt=Taishi, Osaka|1=Taishi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. The mound is part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and has a square (''hōfun'') type. Although the actual identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 this mound as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, the 31st Emperor of Japan, under the name "'''Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi'''." == Overview == [[File:Kasuga mukaiyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Aerial photograph of Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun
(1985)}}{{small|{{National Land Image Intypeation (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed on a terrace at the southwestern edge of the Kasuga settlement in the Shinagadani area, situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. Currently managed as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}, no full-map_size excavations have been conducted. However, an academic survey was carried out in February 2012.[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2300T_T20C12A2CC0000/ "Research Survey Conducted at Yōmei Mausoleum in Osaka"] (''Nikkei'', February 23, 2012). The mound is square in type and faces south. The structure is estimated to have three tiers, measuring 65 meters east–west, 60 meters north–south, and 10 meters in height.[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] (Taishi Town Website). Surrounding the mound is a moat approximately 7 meters wide, and the entire structure, including the outer embankments, reaches a total size of 100 meters east–west and 90 meters north–south. No {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} have been identified on the mound. The burial facility has not been confirmed. However, historical records from the Edo period, including documents from the Yoshimura family of Kasuga, suggest the possibility of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|lt=yokona-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The ''Byōryōki'' also mentions exposed large stones, potentially remnants of the chamber. The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Its design is noted to share similarities with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|en|Ishibutai Kofun|qid=Q3816159|lt=Ishibutai Kofun|1=Ishibutai Kofun}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|qid=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara|1=Asuka, Nara}}, suggesting a possible connection or shared architectural plan. == Interred_individual == The actual individual buried within the Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Yōmei, who is said to have passed away in 587 and was originally buried at the {{ill|Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja|磐余池上陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iware Ikeue Mausoleum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before being reinterred at this site.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency). The mound represents one of the earliest square-shaped imperial tombs, marking a significant transition in the design of imperial burials during this period. == Related Sites == The Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun is part of the "Umebachi Mausoleums," a group of five imperial tombs in the Shinagadani Kofun Group, which also includes the tombs of Emperors Bidatsu, Suiko, and Kōtoku, as well as the burial site of Prince Shōtoku. == References == '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="primary"}} '''Secondary Sources''' {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=National History Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6426904}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4346755}}|year=|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17213509}} Vol. 28: Osaka Prefecture|editor=|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|year=1986|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Heibonsha}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=The Valley of Royal Mausoleums: Shinagadani Kofun Group|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|ref={{Harvid|Royal Valley}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|qid=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei|1=Emperor Yōmei}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/031/ Kawachi Shinagano Hara no Misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/yomeitennoryo.html Emperor Yōmei Mausoleum] - Taishi Town Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|春日向山古墳|version=93995111|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:春日向山古墳]] {{Commons category|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11513590}} [[Category:Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taishi, Ōsaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
